response: We present a new method for [task/problem], evaluated against a strong baseline across five random seeds on the test set. Our approach achieves an average accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1%, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9% for the baseline, yielding an absolute improvement of +1.2%. A two-sample t-test indicates that this difference is not statistically significant (t = 1.04, p = 0.31), with a 95% confidence interval on the gain of [–1.1, +3.5]. These results suggest that, while our method shows a modest numerical advantage, the observed improvement may be attributable to random variation and does not conclusively demonstrate superiority over the baseline. Our findings highlight the importance of careful statistical assessment when benchmarking incremental improvements for [task/problem], and we discuss directions for future work to further validate and strengthen these gains.
